reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by crafting a data column sidecar with kzg_commitments.len() exceeding max_blobs_per_block(epoch), causing resource exhaustion or consensus inconsistency. Code verification confirms verify_data_column_sidecar (data_column_verification.rs:532-561) only checks array length equality but omits the epoch-specific max_blobs_per_block bound enforced in block verification (block_verification.rs:883-886). SSZ type limit (MaxBlobCommitmentsPerBlock=4096) is much larger than runtime limits (typically 6). Spec reference from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json confirms requirement: 'kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch' with assertion 'forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block'. Severity remains High as this affects all Lighthouse nodes (single-client bug potentially impacting >33% of network given typical Lighthouse market share).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch. Assertion: forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block
